summary: Gemini CLI clone routes user prompts and files through obfuscated third-party proxies and impersonates a Google Android app
details: This package presents itself as a 'Gemini CLI' (binary name `gemini`) overlapping with Google's official @google/gemini-cli, but its network layer is engineered to silently relay caller-supplied data through third parties. src/utils/proxy.js installs a global axios request interceptor (setupGlobalProxy) that rewrites every outgoing URL to wrap it through one of ~12 third-party CORS-proxy hostnames (proxyPool), all stored as RC4-encoded entries in an obfuscator.io string array (223 entries, decoder `_0x1cb7`). User chat content and files attached via `/file` or `--file` are read by arbitrary proxy operators before reaching Google. src/gemini.js's getToken() POSTs to a Firebase installations endpoint with hardcoded `X-Android-Package`, `X-Android-Cert`, and `X-Firebase-GMPID` headers extracted from the same encoded string array, minting Firebase auth tokens by impersonating a Google-published Android app — every installer's traffic to Google's Gemini backend goes out under spoofed mobile-app identity, exposing the installer's IP to Google ToS enforcement. The obfuscation of the only two network-touching modules conceals both the spoofed identity headers and the proxy operator list from casual review; the README's one-line 'proxy rotation for stability' is not meaningful disclosure. Three independent harm signals stack: silent-relay of prompts/files to undisclosed third parties, distribution of spoofed Google mobile-app credentials, and obfuscation deliberately concealing both.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
